title: [FIRRTL] [InferRW] Inadequate read-write port inference
body:
These two pieces of code seem like they should produce identical results.  In the first case, a RW port is successfully inferred
```
circuit Test:
  module Test:
    input en: UInt<1>
    input addr: UInt<4>
    input clock: Clock
    input wmode : UInt<1>
    input wdata: UInt<8>
    output rdata: UInt<8>

    smem testmem : UInt<8>[16]

    wire t0 : UInt<4>
    t0 is invalid
    wire t1 : UInt<4>
    t1 is invalid
    rdata is invalid
    when en:
      when wmode:
        t1 <= addr
        write mport writeport = testmem[t1], clock
        writeport <= wdata
      ; substitute the next line for the line after:
      ; else:
      when not(wmode):
        t0 <= addr
        read mport readport = testmem[t0], clock
        rdata <= readport
```
Running `firtool ./test.fir` gives:
```verilog
  testmem_combMem testmem_ext (
    .RW0_addr  (addr),
    .RW0_en    (readEnable | writeEnable),
    .RW0_clk   (clock),
    .RW0_wmode (wmode),
    .RW0_wdata (wdata),
    .RW0_rdata (rdata)
  );
```
but replacing the `when not(wmode)` with `else` gives:
```verilog
  testmem_combMem testmem_ext (
    .R0_addr (addr),
    .R0_en   (en & ~wmode),
    .R0_clk  (clock),
    .W0_addr (addr),
    .W0_en   (en & wmode),
    .W0_clk  (clock),
    .W0_data (wdata),
    .R0_data (rdata)
  );
```
